questions.question: What is the definition of speed?
questions.correct_answer: Distance covered in a unit time
questions.explanation: Speed is the rate at which an object covers a distance.

questions.question: What are the different units of speed?
questions.correct_answer: Meter per second, Kilometer per hour
questions.explanation: The C.G.S. unit of speed is meter per second, while the S.I. unit is kilometer per hour.

questions.question: What is the formula for speed?
questions.explanation: The formula for speed is distance divided by time.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.explanation: Uniform motion is a type of motion where the object moves with constant velocity.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.explanation: Non-uniform motion is a type of motion where the object moves with variable velocity.

questions.question: What is the difference between speed and velocity?
questions.correct_answer: Speed is scalar, while velocity is vector
questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity that represents the rate at which an object covers a distance, while velocity is a vector quantity that represents the rate of change of displacement.

questions.question: What is acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.

questions.question: What are the different types of acceleration?
questions.explanation: There are four types of accelerations: uniform acceleration, non-uniform acceleration, tangential acceleration, and normal acceleration.

questions.question: What is the relationship between speed, velocity, and acceleration?
questions.explanation: The relationship between speed, velocity, and acceleration is given by the formula speed equals velocity divided by time.
questions.source_section: Section 9: Relationship Between Speed, Velocity, and Acceleration

questions.question: What are some real-life examples of motion?
questions.explanation: Some real-life examples of motion include walking, running, throwing a ball.

notes.title: Definition of Speed
notes.content: Speed is the rate at which an object covers a distance. It is a scalar quantity and has no direction.

notes.title: Units of Speed
notes.content: The C.G.S. unit of speed is meter per second, while the S.I. unit is kilometer per hour.

notes.title: Formula for Speed
notes.content: The formula for speed is distance divided by time.

notes.title: Uniform Motion
notes.content: Uniform motion is a type of motion where the object moves with constant velocity.

notes.title: Non-Uniform Motion
notes.content: Non-uniform motion is a type of motion where the object moves with variable velocity.

facts.fact: The fastest land animal is the cheetah, which can reach speeds of up to 120 km/h.
The fastest man-made object ever created is the International Space Station, which orbits the Earth at a speed of approximately 27,400 km/h.
The longest recorded flight of a chicken is 13 seconds.
